Package jch.lib.db.snowflake

Class SnowflakeDbScour

  • java.lang.Object
    • jch.lib.db.snowflake.SnowflakeDbScour
  • public class SnowflakeDbScour
extends java.lang.Object
    A collection of methods that allows for a SQL Server target database to be copied in both schema and data to a designated to Snowflake destination.

    TODO: Class needs to be broken up and a refactored reduce megamoth smell.
    "Stands for MEGA MOnolithic meTHod. Often contained inside a God Object, and usually stretches over two screens in height. Megamoths of greater size than 2k LOC have been sighted. Beware of the MEGAMOTH!"
    https://blog.codinghorror.com/new-programming-jargon/

    TODO: For Windows OS, be sure to add mssql-jdbc_auth-9.4.0.x64.dll to C:\Windows\System32\ to allow SQL Server integrated security to work. The file is located in the following location: *\dependencies\sqljdbc_9.4\enu\auth\x64\mssql-jdbc_auth-9.4.0.x64.dll 
        
   _____________________________________________________________________________________________      
                                              |                        |                        |
   SQL Server Host                            | Azure Instance         | Snowflake Instance     |
   ___________________________________________|________________________|________________________|
                                              |                        |                        |
   SQL Server Instance   Extract Stage Folder | Blob Container         | Compute Warehouse      |
        __________        __                  |    __                  |                        |
       /          \      |   \____________    |   |   \____________    |                        |
      |\__________/|     |   ______       \   |   |   ______       \   |      ________________  |
      |            |     |  |      |      |   |   |  |      |      |   |     /               /  | 
      |  Database  |------->| Tbl  |---------------->| Tbl  |-------------->/ @azure_stage  /   |
      |   Schema   |     |  | .gz  |      |   |   |  | .gz  |      |   |   /_______________/    |
      |   Tables   |     |  |______|      |   |   |  |______|      |   |          |             |
       \__________/      |________________|   |   |________________|   |          |             |
                                              |                        |      ____|_____        |
                                              |                        |     /    V     \       |
                   ______                     |                        |    |\__________/|      |
                  | Azr  |                    |                        |    |            |      |
       ---------->| Blb  |------------------->|https                   |    |  Database  |      |
    ___|__        |.json |                    |                        |    |   Schema   |      |
   | ETL  |       |______|                    |                        |    |   Tables   |      |
   | Cfg  |                                   |                        |     \__________/       |
   |.json |                                   |                        |                        |
   |______|        ______                     |                        |                        |
       |          | SF   |                    |                        |                        | 
       ---------->| Crd  |-------------------------------------------->|https                   | 
                  |.json |                    |                        |                        |
                  |______|                    |                        |                        |
                                              |                        |                        |
   ___________________________________________|________________________|________________________|
 
 
     Main ETL Config File:
        snowflake_etl.json
 	
<file>
{
	"etlTitle":"ARCU to Snowflake ETL",
	"baseDir":"C:\\temp\\Snowflake\\",
	
	"ssMaxValue":
		{
			"maxMethod":"SnowflakeDbScour.getSsMaxValue",
			"maxSqlHost":"arcu",
			"maxDatabase":"ARCUSYM000",
			"maxSchema":"dbo",
			"maxTable":"ACCOUNT",
			"maxValueLimiterCol":"PROCESSDATE"
			
		},
	
	"QLog":
		{
			"qlogBaseFileName":"Snowflake_Log_",
			"qlogCharLimit":2000,
			"qlogPrintConsole":true
		},	
	"ETLs":
	[
		{
			"etlName":"ARCUDWdbo",
			"maxFileSize":1000000000,
			"srcSqlHost":"arcu",
			"srcDatabase":"ARCUDW",
			"srcSchema":"dbo",
			"sfCredsLoc":"H:\\snowflake_creds.json",
			"sfDatabase":"ARCUDW",
			"sfSchema":"dbo",
			"rowDelim":"\r\n",
			"colDelim":"~",
			"textQualifier":"\"",
			"escapeChar":"\\",
			"timeDimensions":["ProcessDateKey"]
			"strictPK":false,
			"optionalFull":true,
			"azCredsLoc":"H:\\azure_blob_arcudwdbo.json",
			"azBlobDir":"",
			"sfStage":"@stage_arcudwdbo_dev"
		},
		{
			"etlName":"ARCUSYM000arcu",
			"maxFileSize":1000000000,
			"srcSqlHost":"arcu.firstmarkcu.org",
			"srcDatabase":"ARCUSYM000",
			"srcSchema":"arcu",
			"sfCredsLoc":"T:\\Snowflake\\Config\\snowflake_creds.json",
			"sfDatabase":"ARCUSYM000",
			"sfSchema":"arcu",
			"rowDelim":"\r\n",
			"colDelim":"~",
			"textQualifier":"\"",
			"escapeChar":"\\",
			"timeDimensions":["POSTDATE","PROCESSDATE","DATE"],
			"strictPK":true,
			"optionalFull":true,
			"azCredsLoc":"T:\\Snowflake\\Config\\azure_blob_arcusym000arcu.json",
			"azBlobDir":"",
			"sfStage":"@stage_arcusym000arcu_dev"
		}
	]
}
</file>
  
  
 Azure Blob Config File(s):
        azure_blob_arcusym000dbo.json
        azure_blob_arcusym000arcu.json
        azure_blob_cfsconnectors.json
        azure_blob_analyticscu.json
 	
<file>
{
	"HTTPSStorageHost":"https://sfdevblob.blob.core.windows.net", 
	"Container":"arcusym000dbo", 
	"ContainerSAS":"sp=<SAS>&spr=https&sv=<SAS>&sr=<SAS>&sig=<SAS"
}
</file>     
      
      
 Snowflake Credentials File:
        snowflake_creds.json
 	
<file>
{
	"user":"ARCUETL", 
	"password":"SECRET", 
	"account":"pg73514", 
	"db":"ANALYTICSTEST", 
	"schema":"cu"
	"cnstring":"jdbc:snowflake://pg73514.east-us-2.azure.snowflakecomputing.com"
}
</file>
    File Sizing Best Practices and Limitations
    For best load performance and to avoid size limitations, consider the following data file sizing guidelines. Note that these recommendations apply to bulk data loads as well as continuous loading using Snowpipe.

    General File Sizing Recommendations
    The number of load operations that run in parallel cannot exceed the number of data files to be loaded. To optimize the number of parallel operations for a load, we recommend aiming to produce data files roughly 100-250 MB (or larger) in size compressed. https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-considerations-prepare.html#label-data-load-file-sizing-best-practices

    Author:
    harrisonc

      static void copyApiTableData​(java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String database, java.lang.String schema, java.lang.String table)
      A method that copies data directly from SQL server to Snowflake for a matching DATABASE.SCHEMA.TABLE.
      static void copyApiTableData​(java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase, java.lang.String srcSchema, java.lang.String srcTable, java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String sfDatabase, java.lang.String sfSchema, java.lang.String sfTable)
      Bypasses Blob storage staging and uses direct INSERT statements to fill in data from SQL Server table to source to Snowflake.
      static void copyFullSqlServerDatabase​(java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String srcHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase)
      Creates a copy of database, schema, and tables from a SQL Server host to a Snowflake instance
      static void copySqlServerAllDatabaseSchemas​(java.sql.Connection snowflakeCn, java.lang.String srcHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase)
      Copies schemas from a source SQL Server host to a Snowflake instance
      static void copySqlServerAllDatabaseTables​(java.sql.Connection snowflakeCn, java.lang.String srcHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase)
      Copies schemas from a source SQL Server host to a Snowflake instance by performing a syntax conversion Creates a series of create statements Currently supported: Column Names, Data Types, Default Values, Nullable, and Primary Keys TODO: Method should be refactored moving from test case to
      static void copySqlServerDatabase​(java.sql.Connection snowflakeCn, java.lang.String srcHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase)
      A method that copies a SQL Server Database
      static boolean createTableFromSs​(java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase, java.lang.String srcSchema, java.lang.String srcTable)
      Creates a table in Snowflake by referencing a SQL Server Table.
      static javax.sql.RowSet executeRowSetSnowflakeCommand​(java.lang.String sqlCommand, java.sql.Connection sfConnection)
      A generic Snowflake SQL command execution end-point
      static java.lang.String fileNamePrep​(java.lang.String value, java.lang.String datatypeCat)
      Converts a Postdate String (ie,"12/31/2021") or Processdate (ie,"20221231") or String with special characters to a file system friendly value to help with file naming.

      static java.lang.String sqlCreateTableFromSs​(java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase, java.lang.String srcSchema, java.lang.String srcTable)
      Connect to an existing SQL server and Generates a CREATE TABLE for Snowflake Schema columns used from SQL Server DATA_TYPE_CAT,DATA_TYPE,COLUMN_NAME,NUMERIC_PRECISION,NUMERIC_SCALE, ORDINAL_POSITION,COLUMN_DEFAULT,CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH TODO: Using double quotes make objects case sensitive; give option to use use double quotes or not
      static java.util.ArrayList<java.lang.String> toCols​(javax.sql.RowSet rowSet)
      Returns a unique set of column names for a RowSet that contains "COLUMN_NAME" in caps
      static void writeCsvFromSqlServerAllTablesDiff​(java.lang.String filePath, long maxFileSize, java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase, java.lang.String srcSchema, java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String sfDatabase, java.lang.String sfSchema, java.lang.String rowDelim, java.lang.String colDelim, java.lang.String textQualifier, java.lang.String escapeChar, java.util.ArrayList<java.lang.String> timeDimensions, boolean strictPK, boolean optionalFull, java.lang.String azCredsLoc, java.lang.String azBlobDir, java.lang.String sfStage)
      Connects to a Sql Server with a Database and Schema context, iterates through all tables and compares a table against a given Snowflake Database and Schema context with a table of the same name, and creates a compressed CSV file of the difference between the two tables.
      static void writeCsvFromSqlServerAllTablesFull​(java.lang.String filePath, long maxFileSize, java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase, java.lang.String srcSchema, java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String sfDatabase, java.lang.String sfSchema, java.lang.String rowDelim, java.lang.String colDelim, java.lang.String textQualifier, java.lang.String escapeChar, java.lang.String azCredsLoc, java.lang.String azBlobDir, java.lang.String sfStage)
      Connects to a Sql Server with a Database and Schema context, iterates through all tables and compares a table against a given Snowflake Database and Schema context with a table of the same name, and creates a compressed CSV file of the full table within the SQL S Once the file has been created, the file will be sent to a specified Azure Blob Storage container.
      static void writeCsvFromSqlServerTableDiff​(java.lang.String filePath, java.lang.String fileName, long maxFileSize, java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase, java.lang.String srcSchema, java.lang.String srcTable, java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String sfDatabase, java.lang.String sfSchema, java.lang.String sfTable, java.lang.String rowDelim, java.lang.String colDelim, java.lang.String textQualifier, java.lang.String escapeChar, java.lang.String valueLimiterCol, java.lang.String dataTypeCat, java.lang.String sfMaxFromTable, java.lang.String azCredsLoc, java.lang.String azBlobDir, java.lang.String sfStage)
      Establishes time range to create differential extracts for specific table

      static void writeCsvFromSqlServerTableValueLimit​(java.lang.String filePath, java.lang.String fileName, long maxFileSize, java.lang.String srcSqlHost, java.lang.String srcDatabase, java.lang.String srcSchema, java.lang.String srcTable, java.lang.String sfCredsLoc, java.lang.String sfDatabase, java.lang.String sfSchema, java.lang.String sfTable, java.lang.String rowDelim, java.lang.String colDelim, java.lang.String textQualifier, java.lang.String escapeChar, java.lang.String valueLimiterCol, java.lang.String valueLimit, java.lang.String azCredsLoc, java.lang.String azBlobDir, java.lang.String sfStage, boolean sfForceReload)
      Takes source table and creates a *.csv or *.csv.gz based on a source Sql Server host, database, schema, and table, compares to specified remote Snowfalke instance, compute, database, schema, and table to build column ordinal sensitive datasets with optionaly compressing the file via GZip.

      00_CREATE.sql 01_DELETE.sql 02_COPYINTO.sql

    • Method Detail

      • processArgs

        public static void processArgs​(java.lang.String[] args)
        To be ran from Main method to process argument options 
        
 -r <JSON Path>:	Reads JSON config file and prints filled in values.  Fails if JSON isn't parseable.
 -j <JSON Path>:	Runs JSON config file and prints filled in values.  Fails if JSON isn't parseable.
        Example .bat file run: "c:\Java\jdk-11.0.14.1+1-jre\bin\java" -Xmx13048M -Dfile.encoding=Cp1252 -classpath "T:\Snowflake\Snowflake ETL\bin";"T:\Snowflake\Snowflake ETL\dependencies\*"; "T:\Snowflake\Snowflake ETL\dependencies\SnowFlake v3.13.12\*"; "T:\Snowflake\Snowflake ETL\dependencies\sqljdbc_9.4\enu\*"; "T:\Snowflake\Snowflake ETL\dependencies\google-api-client-assembly-1.30.2-1.30.2\google-api-java-client\libs\*" jch.lib.Main -j "T:\Snowflake\Config\snowflake_etl.json"
        Parameters:
        args - ELT Arguments for parsing

      • runFullEtl

        public static void runFullEtl​(java.lang.String baseDir)
        Hard-coded function calls to make up an ETL set
        Parameters:
        baseDir - Staging directory for extracts to be temporarily

      • readJsonFullEtl

        public static void readJsonFullEtl​(java.lang.String jsonBaseDir)
        Displays all defined values in a validly defined JSON file. Runs when -r option is a passed along with a file path to the JSON file. Fails if not JSON file is not valid.
        Parameters:
        jsonBaseDir - Location of main JSON ETL config file

        public static void runJsonFullEtl​(java.lang.String jsonBaseDir)

        Parameters:

      • showVals

        public static void showVals()
        Prin

      • writeCsvFromSqlServerAllTablesFull

        public static void writeCsvFromSqlServerAllTablesFull​(java.lang.String filePath,
                                                      long maxFileSize,
                                                      java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                                      java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                                      java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                                      java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                                      java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                                      java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                                      java.lang.String rowDelim,
                                                      java.lang.String colDelim,
                                                      java.lang.String textQualifier,
                                                      java.lang.String escapeChar,
                                                      java.lang.String azCredsLoc,
                                                      java.lang.String azBlobDir,
                                                      java.lang.String sfStage)
        Connects to a Sql Server with a Database and Schema context, iterates through all tables and compares a table against a given Snowflake Database and Schema context with a table of the same name, and creates a compressed CSV file of the full table within the SQL S Once the file has been created, the file will be sent to a specified Azure Blob Storage container. TODO: suspend warehouse after complete
        Parameters:
        filePath - Output file location. Include last slash (ie, "C:\\temp\\" which becomes "C:\temp\)
        maxFileSize - Max file size threshold (ie, 30000 "means 30Kb" or 4000000000L "means 4Gb")
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C:\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Snowflake database name (ie, "DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        rowDelim - Output file row delimiter (ie,"\r\n" or "\n")
        colDelim - Output file column/field delimiter(ie, "," or "~" or "\t")
        textQualifier - String, text, or VARCHAR datatype value qualifier (ie,"\"" to ")
        escapeChar - POSIX control character (ie, "\\" to \)
        azCredsLoc - Location of Azure credentials of an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "C:\\azure_creds.json")
        azBlobDir - A directory to store extracts for an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "init" or "20220203" or "test/2020203")
        sfStage - The name of the defined stage in Snowflake to consume from Azure (ie, "@stage_azure")

      • writeCsvFromSqlServerAllTablesDiff

        public static void writeCsvFromSqlServerAllTablesDiff​(java.lang.String filePath,
                                                      long maxFileSize,
                                                      java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                                      java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                                      java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                                      java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                                      java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                                      java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                                      java.lang.String rowDelim,
                                                      java.lang.String colDelim,
                                                      java.lang.String textQualifier,
                                                      java.lang.String escapeChar,
                                                      java.util.ArrayList<java.lang.String> timeDimensions,
                                                      boolean strictPK,
                                                      boolean optionalFull,
                                                      java.lang.String azCredsLoc,
                                                      java.lang.String azBlobDir,
                                                      java.lang.String sfStage)
        Connects to a Sql Server with a Database and Schema context, iterates through all tables and compares a table against a given Snowflake Database and Schema context with a table of the same name, and creates a compressed CSV file of the difference between the two tables. The file names will contain the specific time slice the file represents in YYYYMMDD format. Once the file has been created, the file will be sent to a specified Azure Blob Storage container. 
        
                                                Max: 12/31/2021
 SqlServer Hostname -> Database -> dbo -> Transaction(Postdate, TransAmount,.....)
                                                          |
                                                          |-- dbo.Transaction_20201231.csv
                                                          |     |--> dbo.Transaction_20201231.csv.gz to Azure Blob Container
                                                          |
                                                          |-- dbo.Transaction_20201230.csv
                                                          |     |--> dbo.Transaction_20201230.csv.gz to Azure Blob Container
                                                          |
 Snowflake Instance -> Database -> dbo -> Transaction(Postdate, TransAmount,.....)
                                                Max: 12/29/2021
        If the source table contains data but the destination table does not, it will print the full table. If the table doesn't have a time dimension to compare, an optional flag can be specified to print the full table. A full table will be split based on the max file size specified in its uncompressed version of itself 
                dbo.Transaction_001.csv.gz
        dbo.Transaction_002.csv.gz
        dbo.Transaction_***.csv.gz
        Once an extract file has been created and compressed, it then gets sent off to an azure blob storage This method uses a thread pool to concurrently pull tables, with the max concurrency specified by MAX_CONCURRENCY_LEVEL (ie 4 threads at any give time) TODO: suspend warehouse after complete
        Parameters:
        filePath - Output file location (ie, "C:\\temp\\")
        maxFileSize - Max file size threshold (ie, 30000 means 30Kb or 4000000000L means 4Gb)
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Snowflake database name (ei, "DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        rowDelim - Output file row delimiter (ie,"\r\n" or "\n")
        colDelim - Output file column/field delimiter(ie, "," or "~" or "\t")
        textQualifier - String, text, or VARCHAR datatype value qualifier (ie,"\"" to ")
        escapeChar - POSIX control character (ie, "\\" to \)
        timeDimensions - ArrayList of Strings for a list of fields in which to look for to split table up along a dimension of time (ie, new ArrayList<String>(Arrays.asList("POSTDATE", "PROCESSDATE", "DATE")))
        strictPK - Looks for the value "PK" in column constraint in conjunction timeDimnensios arrayList if set true
        optionalFull - Prints full file it time dimension can't be determined it set to true
        azCredsLoc - Location of Azure credentials of an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "C:\\azure_creds.json")
        azBlobDir - A directory to store extracts for an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "init" or "20220203" or "test/2020203")
        sfStage - The name of the defined stage in Snowflake to consume from Azure (ie, "@stage_azure")

      • writeCsvFromSqlServerTableDiff

        public static void writeCsvFromSqlServerTableDiff​(java.lang.String filePath,
                                                  java.lang.String fileName,
                                                  long maxFileSize,
                                                  java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                                  java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                                  java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                                  java.lang.String srcTable,
                                                  java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                                  java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                                  java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                                  java.lang.String sfTable,
                                                  java.lang.String rowDelim,
                                                  java.lang.String colDelim,
                                                  java.lang.String textQualifier,
                                                  java.lang.String escapeChar,
                                                  java.lang.String valueLimiterCol,
                                                  java.lang.String dataTypeCat,
                                                  java.lang.String sfMaxFromTable,
                                                  java.lang.String azCredsLoc,
                                                  java.lang.String azBlobDir,
                                                  java.lang.String sfStage)
        Establishes time range to create differential extracts for specific table 
        
                                                Max: 12/31/2021
 SqlServer Hostname -> Database -> dbo -> Transaction(Postdate, TransAmount,.....)
                                                          |
                                                          |-- dbo.Transaction_20201231.csv
                                                          |     |--> dbo.Transaction_20201231.csv.gz to Azure Blob Container
                                                          |
                                                          |-- dbo.Transaction_20201230.csv
                                                          |     |--> dbo.Transaction_20201230.csv.gz to Azure Blob Container
                                                          |
 Snowflake Instance -> Database -> dbo -> Transaction(Postdate, TransAmount,.....)
                                                Max: 12/29/2021
        If the source table contains data but the destination table does not, it will print the full table. If the table doesn't have a time dimension to compare, an optional flag can be specified to print the full table. A full table will be split based on the max file size specified in its uncompressed version of itself 
                dbo.Transaction_001.csv.gz
        dbo.Transaction_002.csv.gz
        dbo.Transaction_***.csv.gz
        TODO: suspend warehouse after complete
        Parameters:
        filePath - Output file location. Include last slash (ie, "C:\\temp\\" which becomes "C:\temp\)
        fileName - Output base file name. Do not include file extension. (ie, "dbo.ACCOUNT")
        maxFileSize - Max file size threshold (ie, 30000 "means 30Kb" or 4000000000L "means 4Gb")
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Snowflake database name (ei, "DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        sfTable - Remote Snowflake table name to compare columns (ie,"ACCOUNT")
        rowDelim - Output file row delimiter (ie,"\r\n" or "\n")
        colDelim - Output file column or field delimiter(ie, "," or "~" or "\t")
        textQualifier - String, text, or VARCHAR datatype value qualifier (ie,"\"")
        escapeChar - POSIX control character (ie, "\\")
        valueLimiterCol - Column or Field Name used to limit dataset (ie, "ProcessDate" or "POSTDATE")
        dataTypeCat - Value (ie, int: "20220203" or date: "2/3/2022" or date: "2022/02/03")
        sfMaxFromTable - Table name for which to get max value for valueLimiterCol
        azCredsLoc - Location of the Azure credentials of an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "C\\azure_creds.json")
        azBlobDir - A directory to store extracts for an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "init" or "20220203" or "test/2020203")
        sfStage - The name of the defined stage in Snowflake to consume from Azure (ie, "@stage_azure")

      • writeCsvFromSqlServerTableValueLimit

        public static void writeCsvFromSqlServerTableValueLimit​(java.lang.String filePath,
                                                        java.lang.String fileName,
                                                        long maxFileSize,
                                                        java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                                        java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                                        java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                                        java.lang.String srcTable,
                                                        java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                                        java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                                        java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                                        java.lang.String sfTable,
                                                        java.lang.String rowDelim,
                                                        java.lang.String colDelim,
                                                        java.lang.String textQualifier,
                                                        java.lang.String escapeChar,
                                                        java.lang.String valueLimiterCol,
                                                        java.lang.String valueLimit,
                                                        java.lang.String azCredsLoc,
                                                        java.lang.String azBlobDir,
                                                        java.lang.String sfStage,
                                                        boolean sfForceReload)
        Takes source table and creates a *.csv or *.csv.gz based on a source Sql Server host, database, schema, and table, compares to specified remote Snowfalke instance, compute, database, schema, and table to build column ordinal sensitive datasets with optionaly compressing the file via GZip. TODO: suspend warehouse after complete
        Parameters:
        filePath - Output file location. Include last slash (ie, "C:\\temp\\" which becomes "C:\temp\)
        fileName - Output file name (ie, "dbo.ACCOUNT")
        maxFileSize - Max file size threshold (ie, 30000 "means 30Kb" or 4000000000L "means 4Gb")
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Snowflake database name (ei, "DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        sfTable - Remote Snowflake table name to compare columns (ie,"ACCOUNT")
        rowDelim - Output file row delimiter (ie,"\r\n" or "\n")
        colDelim - Output file column or field delimiter(ie, "," or "~" or "\t")
        textQualifier - String, text, or VARCHAR datatype value qualifier (ie,"\"")
        escapeChar - POSIX control character (ie, "\\")
        valueLimiterCol - Column or Field Name used to limit dataset (ie, "ProcessDate" or "POSTDATE")
        valueLimit - Value (ie, int: "20220203" or date: "2/3/2022" or date: "2022/02/03")
        azCredsLoc - Location of the Azure credentials of an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "C\\azure_creds.json")
        azBlobDir - A directory to store extracts for an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "init" or "20220203" or "test/2020203")
        sfStage - The name of the defined stage in Snowflake to consume from Azure (ie, "@stage_azure")
        sfForceReload - Performs delete on full table if true

      • writeCsvFromSqlServerTableFull

        public static void writeCsvFromSqlServerTableFull​(java.lang.String filePath,
                                                  java.lang.String fileName,
                                                  long maxFileSize,
                                                  java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                                  java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                                  java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                                  java.lang.String srcTable,
                                                  java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                                  java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                                  java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                                  java.lang.String sfTable,
                                                  java.lang.String rowDelim,
                                                  java.lang.String colDelim,
                                                  java.lang.String textQualifier,
                                                  java.lang.String escapeChar,
                                                  java.lang.String azCredsLoc,
                                                  java.lang.String azBlobDir,
                                                  java.lang.String sfStage)
        TODO: suspend warehouse after complete
        Parameters:
        filePath - Output file location (ie, "C:\\temp\\")
        fileName - Output file name (ie, "dbo.ACCOUNT")
        maxFileSize - Max file size threshold (ie, 30000 is "30Kb" or 4000000000L is "4Gb")
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Snowflake database name (ei, "DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        sfTable - Remote Snowflake table name to compare columns (ie,"ACCOUNT")
        rowDelim - Output file row delimiter (ie,"\r\n" or "\n")
        colDelim - Output file column or field delimiter(ie, "," or "~" or "\t")
        textQualifier - String, text, or VARCHAR datatype value qualifier (ie,"\"")
        escapeChar - POSIX control character (ie, "\\")
        azCredsLoc - Location of the Azure credentials of an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "C\\azure_creds.json")
        azBlobDir - A directory to store extracts for an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "init" or "20220203" or "test/2020203")
        sfStage - The name of the defined stage in Snowflake to consume from Azure (ie, "@stage_azure")

      • getSsMaxValue

        public static java.lang.String getSsMaxValue​(java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                             java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                             java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                             java.lang.String srcTable,
                                             java.lang.String valueLimiterCol)
        Returns the maximum value from a particular DATABASE.SCHEMA.TABLE.COLUMN
        Parameters:
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        valueLimiterCol - Column or Field Name used to limit dataset (ie, "ProcessDate" or "POSTDATE")
        Returns:
        String

      • checkTableAndCols

        public static java.lang.String checkTableAndCols​(java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                                 java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                                 java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                                 java.lang.String srcTable,
                                                 java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                                 java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                                 java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                                 java.lang.String sfTable,
                                                 java.lang.String valueLimiterCol,
                                                 java.lang.String dataTypeCat,
                                                 boolean renameAndCreate)
        Parameters:
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source Table to compare against
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C:\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Snowflake database name (ie, "DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        sfTable - Destination table to compare to
        valueLimiterCol - Column or Field Name used to limit dataset (ie, "ProcessDate" or "POSTDATE")
        dataTypeCat - Looks for "TEXT", "DATETIME", "NUMERIC", or "OTHER"
        renameAndCreate - Renames table, and returns string of new table name if successful
        Returns:
        Return new file name as evidence of success

      • renameTable_Processdated

        public static java.lang.String renameTable_Processdated​(java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                                        java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                                        java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                                        java.lang.String sfTable,
                                                        java.lang.String valueLimiterCol,
                                                        java.lang.String dataTypeCat)
        Renames a table with a provided date context in YYYYMMDD format.
        Example: dbo.ACCOUNT to dbo.ACCOUNT_20211212
        Parameters:
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C:\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Snowflake database name (ie, "DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        sfTable - Destination table to compare to
        valueLimiterCol - Column or Field Name used to limit dataset (ie, "ProcessDate" or "POSTDATE")
        dataTypeCat - Looks for "TEXT", "DATETIME", "NUMERIC", or "OTHER"
        Returns:
        New name for table rename

      • createTableFromSs

        public static boolean createTableFromSs​(java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                        java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                        java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                        java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                        java.lang.String srcTable)
        Creates a table in Snowflake by referencing a SQL Server Table.
        Parameters:
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C:\\snowflake_creds.json")
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        Returns:
        Returns true if no errors were encountered in creating the table

      • writeSqlCreateTable

        public static void writeSqlCreateTable​(java.lang.String extractDir,
                                       java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                       java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                       java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                       java.lang.String srcTable,
                                       java.lang.String azCredsLoc,
                                       java.lang.String azBlobDir)
        Writes CREATE TABLE statements to file, and optionally send to Azure Blob Container.
        Parameters:
        extractDir - Directory location in which to write SQL statement to.
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        azCredsLoc - Location of the Azure credentials of an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "C:\\azure_creds.json")
        azBlobDir - A directory to store extracts for an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "init" or "20220203" or "test/2020203")

      • writeSql

        public static void writeSql​(java.lang.String extractDir,
                            java.lang.String fileName,
                            java.lang.String sql,
                            java.lang.String azCredsLoc,
                            java.lang.String azBlobDir)
        00_CREATE.sql 01_DELETE.sql 02_COPYINTO.sql
        Parameters:
        extractDir - Directory location in which to write SQL statement to, usually the same as extract directory location.
        fileName - File name to save to.
        sql - SQL statement to be save to file.
        azCredsLoc - Location of Azure credentials of an Azure Blob Container instance (ie, "C:\\azure_creds.json")
        azBlobDir - Azure blob directory to store

      • copyApiTableData

        public static void copyApiTableData​(java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                    java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                    java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                    java.lang.String srcTable,
                                    java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                    java.lang.String sfDatabase,
                                    java.lang.String sfSchema,
                                    java.lang.String sfTable)
        Bypasses Blob storage staging and uses direct INSERT statements to fill in data from SQL Server table to source to Snowflake. Very "Snowflake Cloud Services" heavy and much slower for bulk data loads.
        Parameters:
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C:\\snowflake_creds.json")
        sfDatabase - Remote Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        sfSchema - Remote Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        sfTable - Destination table to compare to

      • executeRowSetSnowflakeCommand

        public static javax.sql.RowSet executeRowSetSnowflakeCommand​(java.lang.String sqlCommand,
                                                             java.sql.Connection sfConnection)
        A generic Snowflake SQL command execution end-point
        Parameters:
        sqlCommand - The SQL command to be executed
        sfConnection - An active connection object in which to send the SQL command through
        Returns:
        RowSet

      • setDataTypeCategories

        public static java.util.TreeMap<java.lang.String,​java.lang.String> setDataTypeCategories​(javax.sql.RowSet schema)
        Builds a tree map of Column names to Data Type Category f Key pair is set to upper case
        Parameters:
        schema - A Rowset that contains columns "COLUMN_NAME" and "DATA_TYPE_CAT" produced by SnowflakeDiscovery.sqlDatabaseAllInformationShema(database)
        Returns:
        TreeMap<String, String>:

      • copyApiTableData

        public static void copyApiTableData​(java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                    java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                    java.lang.String database,
                                    java.lang.String schema,
                                    java.lang.String table)
                             throws java.sql.SQLException,
                                    java.io.IOException
        A method that copies data directly from SQL server to Snowflake for a matching DATABASE.SCHEMA.TABLE. *CAUTION* Uses Snowflake Webservices credits Currently not being used as a production item, but leaving available as experimental snowflakeCreds \ --- database, schema, table to match fields srcSQLHost /
        Parameters:
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C:\\snowflake_creds.json")
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        database - Source and destination Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        schema - Source and destination Snowflake schema name to compare columns(ie,"DBO")
        table - Source and destination SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        Throws:
        java.sql.SQLException - desc
        java.io.IOException - desc

      • sfCsvValuePrep

        public static java.lang.String sfCsvValuePrep​(java.lang.String value,
                                              java.lang.String datatypeCat,
                                              java.lang.String textQualifier,
                                              java.lang.String escapeChar)
        Prepares a given value for Snowflake consumption via a CSV based on the supplied datatype. Converts unicode characters to space characters (" ") as Snwoflake complains about trying to consume unicode characters via CSV. Ensure ASCII character range
        Parameters:
        value - Raw input in which to prep for inclusion of a CSV
        datatypeCat - Looks for "TEXT", "DATETIME", "NUMERIC", or "OTHER"
        textQualifier - Character used to wrap around text values, "ie, double quote ", single quote ', etc)
        escapeChar - Escape character to be used if given textQualifier is found in TEXT value (ie, backslash \)
        Returns:
        Prepped value for output

      • fileNamePrep

        public static java.lang.String fileNamePrep​(java.lang.String value,
                                            java.lang.String datatypeCat)
        Converts a Postdate String (ie,"12/31/2021") or Processdate (ie,"20221231") or String with special characters to a file system friendly value to help with file naming.
        Parameters:
        value - File name to prep
        datatypeCat - Looks for "TEXT", "DATETIME", "NUMERIC", or "OTHER"
        Returns:
        String

      • toCols

        public static java.util.ArrayList<java.lang.String> toCols​(javax.sql.RowSet rowSet)
        Returns a unique set of column names for a RowSet that contains "COLUMN_NAME" in caps
        Parameters:
        rowSet - A rowset that contains the column name COLUMN_NAME
        Returns:
        ArrayList<String<: A list of column names

      • rowsetColCompare

        public static java.util.ArrayList<java.lang.String> rowsetColCompare​(javax.sql.RowSet ssRowSet,
                                                                     javax.sql.RowSet sfRowSet)
        Compares two RowSets and returns a matching set of column names from both rowsets
        Parameters:
        ssRowSet - Sql Server RowSet
        sfRowSet - SnowFlake RowSet
        Returns:
        rowsetColCompare: ArrayList<String> An array list of matched columns

      • copyFullSqlServerDatabase

        public static void copyFullSqlServerDatabase​(java.lang.String sfCredsLoc,
                                             java.lang.String srcHost,
                                             java.lang.String srcDatabase)
        Creates a copy of database, schema, and tables from a SQL Server host to a Snowflake instance
        Parameters:
        sfCredsLoc - Location of Snowflake credentials specified via JSON (ie, "C:\\snowflake_creds.json")
        srcHost - Source SQL Server host name (String)
        srcDatabase - Source SQL Server database of the previously specified host name (String)

      • copySqlServerDatabase

        public static void copySqlServerDatabase​(java.sql.Connection snowflakeCn,
                                         java.lang.String srcHost,
                                         java.lang.String srcDatabase)
                                  throws java.sql.SQLException
        A method that copies a SQL Server Database
        Parameters:
        snowflakeCn - A connection object that has an active connection to Snowflake
        srcHost - Source SQL Server host name (String)
        srcDatabase - Source SQL Server database of the previously specified host name (String)
        Throws:
        java.sql.SQLException - desc

      • copySqlServerAllDatabaseSchemas

        public static void copySqlServerAllDatabaseSchemas​(java.sql.Connection snowflakeCn,
                                                   java.lang.String srcHost,
                                                   java.lang.String srcDatabase)
                                            throws java.sql.SQLException
        Copies schemas from a source SQL Server host to a Snowflake instance
        Parameters:
        snowflakeCn - An established Snowflake connection to execute Snowflake SQL statements (java.sql.Connection)
        srcHost - Source SQL Server host name (String)
        srcDatabase - Source SQL Server database of the previously specified host name (String)
        Throws:
        java.sql.SQLException - desc

      • copySqlServerAllDatabaseTables

        public static void copySqlServerAllDatabaseTables​(java.sql.Connection snowflakeCn,
                                                  java.lang.String srcHost,
                                                  java.lang.String srcDatabase)
                                           throws java.sql.SQLException
        Copies schemas from a source SQL Server host to a Snowflake instance by performing a syntax conversion Creates a series of create statements Currently supported: Column Names, Data Types, Default Values, Nullable, and Primary Keys TODO: Method should be refactored moving from test case to
        Parameters:
        snowflakeCn - An active Snowflake connection
        srcHost - Source SQL Server host name
        srcDatabase - Source SQL Server database of the previously specified host name
        Throws:
        java.sql.SQLException - desc

      • sqlCreateTableFromSs

        public static java.lang.String sqlCreateTableFromSs​(java.lang.String srcSqlHost,
                                                    java.lang.String srcDatabase,
                                                    java.lang.String srcSchema,
                                                    java.lang.String srcTable)
        Connect to an existing SQL server and Generates a CREATE TABLE for Snowflake Schema columns used from SQL Server DATA_TYPE_CAT,DATA_TYPE,COLUMN_NAME,NUMERIC_PRECISION,NUMERIC_SCALE, ORDINAL_POSITION,COLUMN_DEFAULT,CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH TODO: Using double quotes make objects case sensitive; give option to use use double quotes or not
        Parameters:
        srcSqlHost - Source SQL Server Hostname or IP address (ie, "SQLSERV01" or "192.168.1.102")
        srcDatabase - Source Database in which table resides (ie,"DatabaseName")
        srcSchema - Source Database schema table resides (ie, "dbo")
        srcTable - Source SQL Server table to create extract of (ie."ACCOUNT")
        Returns:
        SQL CREATE TABLE Statement
